data.title: Celldex a new overweight at Morgan Stanley on urticaria candidate
data.description:
Morgan Stanley has initiated Celldex Therapeutics (NASDAQ:CLDX) at overweight saying that barzolvolimab (barzol), its asset for chronic urticaria (CU), is poised to become a major revenue generator in a decade.

The investment bank has a $46 price target (~131% upside based on March 21 close).

Analyst Judah Frommer said that barzol is an anti-KIT monoclonal antibody that helps to reduce mast cells, adding that phase 2 data in CU showed the candidate has a "potentially market-leading efficacy profile with impressive disease control observed irrespective of prior treatment."

He projects barzol revenue of $1.3B in 2035.

Celldex's only other candidate in the clinic is the bispecific antibody CDX-622 in phase 1 for inflammation and fibrosis, though Frommer sees potential here as well.

While Frommer acknowledges the stock has not fared well over the last year -- it's down ~51% over the period -- due to side effects seen with barzol  and increasingly competitive CU therapy development, those side effects are not unique to barzol. He added there is a huge unmet need in CU which "necessitates multiple lines of therapy and informs acceptable risk/benefit."

In earlier stages of testing, barzol is under investigation for eosinophilic esophagitis, prurigo nodulatis, and atopic dermatitis.

data.title: G City reports Q4 results
data.description:
• G City press release (OTCPK:GZTGF): Q4 FFO ILS 2.65 per share.
  • NOI increased in 2024 by ~4.0% and came to ~ILS 1,734 million

data.title: Microsoft receives Sector Outperform rating as Scotiabank initiates coverage
data.description:
Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) received a Sector Outperform rating by Scotiabank due in part to its position in the artificial intelligence race.

"Based on our fieldwork, 2025 will be a paradigm-shifting year during which customer investments accelerate in AI on Azure and Microsoft 365 Copilot," said Scotia Capital analysts, led by Patrick Colville, in an extensive investor note.

Scotiabank also assigned a $470 price target on the Magnificent Seven stock.

The financial firm finds that about 60% of companies are running foundational models in the public cloud, and Azure is in a key position to benefit from generative AI spending.

"Almost all IT decision makers plan to add additional use cases in 2025," Colville said. "Microsoft's partnership with OpenAI, Inc. and pre-existing customer relationships are the key advantage for Azure in AI. Based on our fieldwork, a slow moderation in growth in Microsoft's traditional public cloud business is the most likely outcome – important for assuaging investor fears that one revenue source is being substituted for another."

Microsoft's annual revenue run rate from its AI business hit $13B in the second quarter of fiscal 2025. Scotiabank estimates this could increase to more than $50B by fiscal year 2027, and serve as a primary driver of Microsoft's medium-term growth.

"We estimate that Microsoft's Azure business saw revenue of ~$56 billion in F24, which we forecast will grow to ~$126 billion by F27," Colville noted. "We think AI services will drive more than 50% of these new Azure dollars, and we expect AI services to rapidly grow from 8% to 33% of total Azure revenue between F24 and F27."

When it comes to Microsoft 365 Copilot, Scotiabank estimates there are 5.5M users today, and they expect it to blossom to 43M by the end of fiscal 2027.

Regarding Copilot Studio, which is Microsoft's platform for building and managing AI agents, Scotiabank also eyes a major revenue opportunity, but believes it will take a year or so before it really ramps up. In its industry checks, only a handful of enterprises had implemented AI agents into production, as most were still in the pilot or pre-pilot phase.

Microsoft's Copilot Studio and Salesforce's (CRM) Agentforce appear to be the two most popular platforms for agentic AI development and deployment.

"Based on our expert discussions, ~50% of CIOs are thinking seriously about use cases for agents in 2025, but only ~30% have set aside line items in their IT budget for piloting and building these agents," Colville noted. "And budgets today for agentic AI are not large enough to move the needle for Microsoft."

Microsoft shares have declined by nearly 9% over the past year. Shares are currently trading at their lowest value since early January 2024.

data.title: Stocks push past weak open to edge higher after positive housing market data
data.description:
Wall Street on Thursday overcame a wobbly start to push higher in morning trade, as some positive economic data on the housing market offset caution a day ahead of a major quarterly options expiration event. 

Markets had rallied on Wednesday on the back of a dovish Federal Reserve.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last +0.20% at 5,686.87 points, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.31% at 17,806.23 points. The blue-chip Dow (DJI) was +0.33% at 42,102.93 points. 

Since closing in correction territory on March 13, the S&P (SP500) has rebounded and is now only -7.63% from its most recent record close. 

Shortly after the opening bell, data from the National Association of Realtors showed total U.S. existing home sales rising 4.2% from January to 4.26M in February, easily beating the estimate of 3.95M.

"Don’t get too excited over the jump in existing home sales (closed contracts). Pending home sales, which represent signed contracts, recently fell to fresh lows. This implies closings soften into Q2," Renaissance Macro Research cautioned on X (formerly Twitter).

The economic calendar also had the Conference Board's monthly leading indicator index update. Long seen as a recession indicator, the index declined further in February. 

The three major averages had opened lower following a negative pre-market session. Market participants tempered some of the euphoria sparked by Fed chair Jerome Powell calling tariff-induced inflation "transitory" on Wednesday.

"There was a quiet start to trading on Thursday following last night’s Fed meeting. But there was a sudden ‘risk off’ move mid-morning which saw global stock indices slump, alongside gold, and a rush into the safe-haven of U.S. Treasury bonds," David Morrison, senior market analyst at Trade Nation, said. 

"There was no obvious trigger for the move. Perhaps it simply indicates skittishness amongst traders. There’s currently a high level of uncertainty prevalent amongst market participants due to all the complex geopolitical factors currently at play, together with the mercurial nature of the Trump administration," he added.

A rally in bonds sparked by the Fed extended to Thursday, sending U.S. Treasury yields lower. The 10-year yield (US10Y) was down 1 basis point to 4.23%, while the shorter-end 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 2 basis points to 3.97%. 

For more, see how Treasury yields have done across the curve on the Seeking Alpha bond page.  

Turning to active stocks, Accenture (ACN) was the top percentage loser on the S&P 500 (SP500), -6.3%, after the IT and consulting services provider said Elon Musk's push for government efficiency had led to a negative impact on sales and revenue.

data.title: Meta AI comes to Europe amidst scrutiny by the bloc's regulators
data.description:
After over a year-long delay, Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) has launched its Meta AI chatbot service in the European Union in the midst of an ongoing regulatory probe by the region's competition watchdogs for possible violations of the bloc's Digital Markets Act. 

The company said it will roll out its AI’s chat function in six European languages in the coming weeks, with "a view to find parity with the U.S." and expanding offerings over time. 

Meta AI would be available in 41 European countries, including those in the EU, as well as 21 overseas territories, the company said.

"It’s taken longer than we would have liked to get our AI technology into the hands of people in Europe as we continue to navigate its complex regulatory system—but we’re glad we’re finally here," the tech giant wrote in its blog on Thursday.

Meta AI will be available for free across Europe through its family of apps—Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, and Messenger, the company said.

At the moment, Meta Al has over 700M monthly active users. The service was launched in the U.S. in 2023.

The move comes as American tech giants continue to navigate constant hurdles by antitrust authorities in the region.

Earlier this month, media reports suggested that Meta (NASDAQ:META) and Apple (AAPL) were expected to be given "modest fines" for not complying with the EU's DMA laws.

Yesterday, Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) was found in breach of DMA for two of its services, and Apple was recommended to take "certain steps" to comply with the EU rules.

President Donald Trump in February threatened tariffs against countries that impose fines on U.S. companies. The EU has denied going against American tech firms and pointed out that those companies are also under investigation in America for unfair practices and stifling competition.

data.title: MBDA to invest in Italy unit to boost missile production
data.description:
MBDA, Europe's leading missile manufacturer, plans to expand production and increase its workforce at its Italian division this year. The move aims to boost output and accelerate delivery times, helping the company maintain its stronghold in the industry while countering emerging competitors.

As global demand for defense systems rises, the European Union has prioritized air and missile defense as a key area for investment among member states. This decision follows the extensive deployment of missiles in the ongoing conflict between Ukraine and Russia.

By reinforcing its operations, MBDA seeks to align with Europe's strategic defense initiatives while strengthening its competitive edge in the evolving market.

data.title: Trump reiterates U.S. crypto push; bitcoin wobbles
data.description:
President Donald Trump repeated Thursday the U.S. will "dominate crypto and the next generation of financial technologies," putting an end to the "regulatory war on crypto and bitcoin (BTC-USD)" under the previous Biden administration.

With the termination of what Trump called the "lawless" Operation Chokepoint 2.0, the president is seeking congressional approval for "landmark" stablecoin and market structure legislation creating "simple, common-sense rules" he said in a prerecorded message for the Digital Asset Summit in New York.

Bitcoin's (BTC-USD) price was looking for direction after Trump's comments, rising 1.1% in the last 24 hours to $85.6K. The token initially dipped when his recording started, before paring losses shortly thereafter.

Trump's remarks come after he signed a long-sought executive order early in the month to create a national strategic bitcoin (BTC-USD) reserve.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Accenture targets $12.55-$12.79 EPS for FY 2025 amid strong AI-driven bookings
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Accenture (ACN) Q2 2025

Management View

  • CEO Julie Sweet highlighted bookings of $20.9 billion for the quarter, with 32 clients surpassing $100 million each in quarterly bookings. Revenue reached $16.7 billion, achieving the top end of the guided range.
  • Sweet noted that Accenture continues to lead in generative AI (Gen AI), securing $1.4 billion in new bookings and generating $600 million in related revenue this quarter.
  • She emphasized the company's strategic focus on digital transformation, citing their role as a reinvention partner in large-scale deals.
  • CFO Angie Park reported a 13.5% operating margin for the quarter and delivered $2.82 in EPS, reflecting a 2% year-over-year growth. Free cash flow reached $2.7 billion, and Accenture returned $2.4 billion to shareholders through repurchases and dividends.

Outlook

  • For Q3 FY 2025, Accenture provided revenue guidance of $16.9 billion to $17.5 billion, assuming a foreign exchange impact of approximately -0.5%.
  • The company updated its full-year FY 2025 guidance for revenue growth to 5%-7% in local currency and projects full-year diluted EPS between $12.55 and $12.79.
  • Park mentioned the company's plan to invest up to $3 billion in acquisitions this fiscal year, aligning with its strategy to enhance capabilities in key growth areas.

Financial Results

  • New bookings for the quarter were $20.9 billion, with consulting bookings at $10.5 billion and managed services bookings at $10.4 billion.
  • Consulting revenues grew by 6% in local currency, while managed services revenues expanded by 11%, driven by double-digit growth in technology and infrastructure managed services.
  • Geographically, the Americas led with 11% growth in local currency, followed by EMEA at 8% and Asia-Pacific at 1%.
  • Gross margin for the quarter was 29.9%, slightly down from the previous year.

Q&A

  • Jason Kupferberg, Bank of America: Questioned visibility into client spending amid broader macro uncertainties. Julie Sweet reiterated that while there is an elevated level of uncertainty globally, Accenture remains focused on positioning itself as a reinvention partner for clients undergoing digital transformation.
  • Tien-Tsin Huang, JPMorgan: Inquired about pricing dynamics and subcontractor costs. CFO Angie Park commented that pricing remains stable but competitive, with subcontractor costs fluctuating based on project demands.
  • Bryan Keane, Deutsche Bank: Asked about the performance of U.S. federal business. CFO Angie Park explained that federal revenue represented about 8% of the company’s total and noted that the company has accounted for some potential impacts in its guidance due to ongoing federal contract reviews.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious interest in Accenture's exposure to macroeconomic uncertainties, particularly in the U.S. federal sector and discretionary project spending.
  • Management’s tone during prepared remarks was confident, emphasizing resilience and growth opportunities, particularly in Gen AI and large-scale transformation deals. In contrast, the Q&A section reflected a more measured tone, addressing concerns over uncertainty and competitive pricing.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management maintained a consistent level of confidence but acknowledged increased global uncertainty.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue for Q2 FY 2025 was $16.7 billion, slightly below the $17.7 billion reported in Q1 FY 2025.
  • Operating margin for Q2 was 13.5%, compared to 16.7% in Q1, reflecting higher investments in strategic areas.
  • New bookings increased from $18.7 billion in Q1 to $20.9 billion in Q2, highlighting strong demand for Accenture’s services.
  • Management’s tone and strategy remained consistent, with a continued focus on large-scale transformation projects and Gen AI initiatives.

Risks and Concerns

  • The company highlighted potential risks from ongoing reviews of federal contracts, which could impact future revenue.
  • Analysts raised concerns about macroeconomic uncertainty and its potential influence on client budgets, particularly in the Americas and Europe.
  • Management acknowledged pricing pressures in a competitive market but emphasized their ability to manage costs and maintain profitability through operational efficiencies.

Final Takeaway

Accenture delivered strong Q2 FY 2025 results, driven by robust bookings and a strategic focus on generative AI and large-scale transformations. Despite increased global uncertainty and pricing pressures, the company raised its full-year guidance, reflecting resilience and confidence in its diversified business model and strategic investments.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Jabil projects $7.5B in AI-related revenue for FY 2025, driven by strong cloud and data center demand
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Jabil Inc. (JBL) Q2 2025

Management View

  • CEO Mike Dastoor highlighted that Q2 2025 delivered $6.7 billion in revenue and $1.94 in core diluted EPS, driven by strong performance in capital equipment, cloud, and data center infrastructure markets. He emphasized the resilience of Jabil’s diversified portfolio and its ability to adapt to market dynamics.
  • CFO Greg Hebard reported core operating income of $334 million with core operating margins at 5%. He noted that the Intelligent Infrastructure segment achieved 18% year-over-year revenue growth, driven by demand in AI-related cloud and data center infrastructure. Connected Living & Digital Commerce saw a 13% year-on-year decline in revenue due to the mobility divestiture.
  • Hebard mentioned that cash flow from operations reached $334 million, with adjusted free cash flow at $261 million for the quarter. He confirmed a share repurchase of 2.5 million shares during Q2.
  • Dastoor announced an expansion into Gujarat, India, focusing on photonics capabilities to support domestic demand and infrastructure. He also highlighted the acquisition of Pharmaceuticals International Inc., which opens a $20 billion addressable market in healthcare.

Outlook

  • The company raised its Intelligent Infrastructure revenue outlook for FY 2025, expecting it to reach $7.5 billion, representing a 40% year-on-year increase in AI-related revenues. This growth reflects increasing demand for servers, racks, and other data center infrastructure.
  • Q3 2025 guidance includes total revenue between $6.7 billion and $7.3 billion, with core diluted EPS expected between $2.08 and $2.48. Intelligent Infrastructure segment revenue is projected to grow 22% year-over-year.
  • FY 2025 guidance now anticipates $27.9 billion in revenue with core operating margins of 5.4%. Core EPS for the year is expected to be $8.95, and free cash flow is forecasted to exceed $1.2 billion.

Financial Results

  • Regulated Industries revenue was $2.7 billion, down 8% year-over-year due to weakness in renewable energy and EV markets. However, core operating margin improved by 20 basis points to 4.8%.
  • Intelligent Infrastructure achieved $2.6 billion in revenue, up 18% year-over-year, with a core operating margin improvement of 110 basis points to 5.3%.
  • Connected Living & Digital Commerce revenue was $1.3 billion, down 13% year-over-year, primarily due to the mobility divestiture. Excluding the divestiture, the segment grew 4% year-over-year.
  • Inventory days increased to 80, slightly above the target range, with normalization expected later in the fiscal year.

Q&A

  • Ruplu Bhattacharya, Bank of America, inquired about the US manufacturing footprint and its readiness to support shifts driven by tariffs. CEO Dastoor explained Jabil’s extensive US presence with 30 sites and capabilities to rapidly scale operations in response to customer needs.
  • Samik Chatterjee, JPMorgan, asked about the drivers of increased AI revenue guidance. Dastoor highlighted strong demand for GPU-engineered racks, liquid-cooled data centers, and silicon photonics products.
  • Mark Delaney, Goldman Sachs, questioned the impact of tariffs on production costs. Dastoor clarified that tariff costs are pass-through and emphasized Jabil’s ability to manage supply chain adjustments effectively.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on the AI-related growth and tariff implications. Questions reflected interest in Jabil’s strategic capabilities and market positioning.
  • Management maintained a confident tone during prepared remarks and Q&A, particularly when discussing AI-driven growth and operational resilience. Phrases like “well positioned” and “remarkable resilience” highlighted their confidence.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Intelligent Infrastructure showed accelerated revenue growth from 5% year-over-year in Q1 to 18% in Q2, supported by AI and data center infrastructure demand. Guidance for AI-related revenue increased from $6.5 billion to $7.5 billion for FY 2025.
  • CFO Hebard noted improved cash flow performance in Q2, with adjusted free cash flow rising to $261 million compared to $226 million in Q1.
  • Management sentiment remained consistent, with a focus on prudence in guidance due to macroeconomic uncertainties.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management flagged continued weakness in EV and renewable energy markets as headwinds. The company is cautious about potential tariff impacts and their effect on customer demand.
  • Analysts raised concerns regarding the sustainability of AI-driven revenue growth and potential margin pressures in the cloud and data center segments.

Final Takeaway

Jabil’s Q2 2025 call underscored the company’s strong performance in Intelligent Infrastructure, driven by AI-related demand, and highlighted a strategic focus on healthcare and photonics. With raised revenue guidance and robust cash flow projections, the company remains confident in navigating market challenges while capitalizing on growth opportunities in data centers and automation.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Exxon protests Colonial Pipeline's proposed changes to fuel shipping terms
data.description:
Exxon Mobil (NYSE:XOM) filed a protest notice asking the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission to block Colonial Pipeline's proposed changes in fuel shipping terms, citing potential harm to shippers and consumers, Reuters reported late Wednesday.

Colonial Pipeline recently sought federal approval to stop shipping different gasoline grades at the same time, and to eliminate shipments of Grade 5 gasoline, saying the changes would streamline its operations and minimize slowdowns.

"Colonial's proposed tariff changes will severely disrupt the efficiency of the gasoline supply chain," Exxon (NYSE:XOM) said in its protest notice.I

f the changes are approved, shippers would not be allowed to ship gasoline products with higher Reid Vapor Pressure, but Colonial could deliver the products to downstream markets; Exxon (XOM) said the proposed changes benefit Colonial but harm margins for everyone else involved in the supply chain.
